rationale: The citing paper uses Novemsky et al. (2007) in its theoretical development of an alternative metacognitive pathway: feelings of decision difficulty can affect choice outcomes. It cites that paper specifically for evidence that incidental difficulty (such as hard-to-read font) increases choice deferral, then argues that assortment-size-related difficulty may similarly influence satisfaction. This is more than a passing bibliography mention because it supports a concrete mechanism the paper tests alongside expectation disconfirmation, even though the paper does not directly extend Novemsky et al.'s full framework.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on the cited paper as prior evidence that subjective processing difficulty can shape choice behavior. It uses that insight to motivate and interpret a parallel overload/difficulty account while distinguishing its own expectation-disconfirmation mechanism.
